query: I want to add a new input to go-ci.yml for enabling govulncheck. Which repos would be affected by this change?
I'm going to remove the codecov input from python-ci.yml since nobody uses it. Can you check if any consumer repos actually pass that input?
What's the blast radius if I change the default node version from 22 to 24 in node-ci.yml?
Which repos currently use the apt-packages input? I want to make sure the step still works after I refactor the install logic.
I need to bump the actions/checkout SHA across all workflows. Before I do, which consumer repos are calling hookshot right now vs still on their old inline CI?
Are there any pending sync PRs from .github that would add hookshot callers to repos that don't have them yet?
copilot-money-mcp's CI is failing after my last change to node-ci.yml. It needs libsecret-1-dev — did I break the apt-packages input?
I want to add a new reusable workflow for Rust projects. How does the .github sync engine decide which caller to seed into a repo?
The actionlint step in ci.yml is failing because I have a syntax error in the scan.yml workflow. Can you help me fix it?
How do I write a composite action that accepts a release tag as input? I'm building a new action for hookshot.
Can you review the cosign-releases action for security issues? I want to make sure the bash is safe.
I need to update the README with better examples for how to use python-ci.yml with poetry projects.
The dependabot-auto-merge workflow isn't merging minor updates. Can you debug why the condition isn't matching?
I want to set up a new public repo under dakaneye/ for a Go CLI tool. What's the standard CI setup?
Can you pin all the action SHAs in go-ci.yml to the latest versions? Some of them are outdated.
I'm adding a typecheck step to python-ci.yml. Should I make it default to true or false?
